articles.title: Trump says US will 'probably run' the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Trump says US will 'probably run' the Strait of Hormuz US President Donald Trump has said that Washington "will probably run" the Strait of Hormuz and that it should "be reimbursed for that". He told Fox News in a phone interview that: “We’re going to keep the Strait and we’ll probably run it. We’ll become ⁠the guardian of ⁠the Strait. Maybe we’ll call it the guardian ⁠angel of the Strait, and we ⁠should be reimbursed for that."

articles.summary: Iran fired ‘warning shots’ at two ships in Hormuz Iranian state TV said on Monday that Iran fired “warning shots” at two ships transiting through the Strait of Hormuz, accusing them of breaching their announcement that the waterway was closed due to the US violations of the ceasefire agreement between the nations. “This morning, two ships that were attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz illegally were targeted and stopped by warning shots fired by the navy of the Revolutionary Guards,” a correspondent based near the Strait announced.
